title: erigeron acris subsp. serotinus
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. serotinus, commonly known as the late flowering hawk's beard or late flowering daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce the risk of infections. Erigeron acris subsp. serotinus can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant for therapeutic purposes.
